title: scenario_train_derailment_fire
description: Freight train derailment with multiple cars on fire and hazmat release risk

timeline.title: Dispatch
timeline.description: Report of a freight train derailment near mile post 44. Multiple cars off the tracks. Fire reported. Rail emergency line contacted.

timeline.title: En Route
timeline.description: Railroad confirms 8 cars derailed. Manifest lists car 4 as ethylene oxide (UN 1040) and car 7 as gasoline (UN 1203). Both cars on fire.

timeline.title: On Scene
timeline.description: Eight freight cars derailed across 600 feet. Cars 3 and 4 are burning. Orange flames from gasoline car 7 visible. Dense smoke. Railroad and EPA en route.

timeline.title: Hazmat Release
timeline.description: Ethylene oxide (car 4) is venting—colorless gas with explosive range of 3-100%. Vapor cloud drifting northeast toward a residential community 0.3 miles away.

timeline.title: Evacuation
timeline.description: Law enforcement evacuating 0.5-mile radius. Hazmat establishing exclusion zone. Water fog being used to disperse vapor cloud. BLEVE risk monitored.

timeline.title: Scene Stabilized
timeline.description: Vapor cloud dispersed. Gasoline car 7 fire extinguished with foam. Ethylene oxide car isolated and monitored by hazmat. Evacuation completed.

node-1.title: Initial Size-Up
node-1.situation: Eight freight cars are derailed. Cars 3 and 7 are burning. The railroad manifest confirms car 4 is ethylene oxide (UN 1040)—flammable, toxic, with an explosive range of 3–100%. Car 4 is venting a colorless vapor cloud drifting northeast toward residential homes 0.3 miles away. You are first on scene with one engine.
node-1.question: What is your immediate priority action?

options.label: A
options.text: Stage upwind at least 1,000 feet, request hazmat and law enforcement for immediate 0.5-mile evacuation downwind, contact the railroad emergency line for the full manifest and CHEMTREC for ethylene oxide guidance, and do NOT apply water to ethylene oxide without hazmat direction.

options.label: B
options.text: Apply water fog immediately to the venting car 4 to knock down the vapor cloud before it reaches the homes.

options.label: C
options.text: Advance to car 7 to extinguish the gasoline fire and prevent it from spreading to car 4.

node-2.title: Evacuation Priority
node-2.situation: Hazmat is 20 minutes out. Law enforcement has 4 units available. The ethylene oxide vapor cloud is visible on air monitoring equipment at 2.5%—within the explosive range. The residential community northeast is 0.3 miles away and has approximately 400 residents.
node-2.question: How do you coordinate the evacuation?

options.label: A
options.text: Direct all 4 law enforcement units to immediate door-to-door evacuation of the residential community downwind; request LRAD or reverse-911 alert; and keep fire crews staged upwind ready to support as hazmat arrives—no fire crew in the hot zone.

options.label: B
options.text: Send 2 fire crew into the hot zone to identify the leak source and manually close the vent valve on car 4.

options.label: C
options.text: Apply water to cool car 4 from maximum distance to reduce venting rate while evacuation proceeds.

node-3.title: Hazmat Operations
node-3.situation: Hazmat has arrived in Level A. Evacuation is 80% complete. Hazmat team advises they can use water fog to disperse the ethylene oxide vapor cloud away from the community. The gasoline car 7 fire is still burning and threatening car 6 (empty lumber). Hazmat requests fire to suppress car 7 while they manage the vapor cloud.
node-3.question: What do you order?

options.label: A
options.text: Coordinate with hazmat: deploy 2.5-inch line with foam concentrate to suppress the gasoline car 7 fire from a safe upwind approach angle identified by hazmat, while hazmat manages vapor cloud dispersion with water fog—unified command with railroad and EPA.

options.label: B
options.text: Apply water only (no foam) to car 7 to avoid introducing a new chemical into the spill area.

options.label: C
options.text: Decline to advance on car 7 since it is within the hazmat exclusion zone.

node-4.title: Scenario Complete
node-4.situation: Gasoline car 7 was extinguished with AFFF foam. Water fog dispersed the ethylene oxide vapor cloud below the explosive range. Evacuation of 400 residents was completed with no casualties. Hazmat in Level A secured the ethylene oxide car. EPA managed environmental containment. Unified command with railroad, hazmat, law enforcement, and fire was established successfully. Your disciplined non-approach, rapid evacuation coordination, and expert-guided fire attack demonstrated best practice for a multi-hazard rail derailment.
node-4.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
